reviewed_files: AI协作项目全生命周期框架.md (v1.5, ~164KB, 2472 lines)
AI协作项目全生命周期框架.json (v1.5, ~88KB, 1913 lines)
AI协作项目全生命周期框架_v1.5_独立审查报告.md (R4 review)
AI协作项目全生命周期框架_v1.5_独立审查报告.json (R4 structured data)
metadata.methodology: Full local-file review with UTF-8 decoding. No web browsing. No GitNexus source inspection.

json_synced.evidence: metadata.version='1.5', metadata.revision='v1.5 (2026-06-14)', evidence_classification section exists with full S/E/I/J/Sp taxonomy, provenance_notes section exists, changelog.v1_5 section exists, metadata.independent_reviews includes R4 entry

metadata_consistent.evidence: MD header says v1.5, MD footer says v1.5 草案, JSON metadata.version=1.5. Caveat: SOP and meta-audit still scoped to v1.4 (acknowledged, not contradictory).
metadata_consistent.residual_issue: Companion SOP (v1.0.2) and meta-audit (v1.0.2) explicitly scope to v1.4, not v1.5. Known lag, not a regression.

provenance_annotated.evidence: MD header includes [I] labels for all 4 v1.5 additions and [S] label for GitNexus source. Explicit caveat: 'GitNexus源文件未随框架分发——独立验证需对照 <GITNEXUS_PROJECT>/'. JSON provenance_notes.gitnexus_v1_5 contains same caveat in structured form.

taxonomy_self_applied.evidence: v1.5 additions carry [I] labels in header. JSON evidence_classification.self_application_status honestly states: 'v1.5新增四节已标注证据类型, 但框架其余部分尚未回溯标注'. distribution_note acknowledges typical distribution targets are [J] heuristic, not empirically established.
taxonomy_self_applied.scope_limitation: Only v1.5 additions are labeled. v1.0-v1.4 content (bulk of document) is not retroactively annotated.

modes_integrated.evidence: §6.3 decision tree includes Mode 8/9 branches. §6.4 upgrade triggers include Mode 8/9 (triggers 1, 3, 5). §6.3 boundary notes (v1.5新增) specify Mode 8 trigger revision and Mode 9 Solo caveat. JSON patterns and upgrade_triggers updated.

additional_design_fixes.mode8_trigger_detail: From 'stage count >= 3' to 'branching dependencies or hidden-coupling risk'
additional_design_fixes.solo_mode_caveat_detail: Solo mode provides role separation but not context independence
additional_design_fixes.blind_first_detail: §9.2 requires cold reading before consulting prior review reports

regressions_found.description: JSON framework_self_governance.usage_tiers.完整版.升级项 does not include Mode 8 or Mode 9. MD §1.4 usage tiers table also omits explicit Mode 8/9 references. Incomplete integration of new modes into the usage tier system.

chatgpt_blind_spots.finding: Claude Code CLI coupling is deeper than acknowledged
chatgpt_blind_spots.detail: Framework is deeply coupled to Claude Code primitives (CLAUDE.md, memory/, /loop, Workflow tool, /session-end skill) at every layer. §11.4 maps these but frames them as one option. ChatGPT-family reviewers, accustomed to OpenAI ecosystem, may not notice how Claude-specific the executable form is.
chatgpt_blind_spots.recommendation: Distinguish conceptual architecture (tool-agnostic) from executable instantiation (Claude Code-specific). Add a Tool Adaptation Guide for Cursor/Windsurf/Qwen Code/Copilot.

chatgpt_blind_spots.finding: JSON companion is a governance artifact, not a data artifact
chatgpt_blind_spots.detail: 88KB JSON tracks review history, open items, death criteria, and evidence taxonomy — useful as governance record but not as a machine-readable project template. ChatGPT reviewers familiar with JSON as configuration may not notice this distinction.
chatgpt_blind_spots.recommendation: A truly useful JSON companion would be a project template schema that a tool could parse to scaffold a new project's Spec/Dev Log/Closure structure.

china_ecosystem_observations.finding: Multi-backend workflow is mainstream Chinese AI practitioner culture
china_ecosystem_observations.detail: Using 5+ LLM backends (Kimi, DeepSeek, GLM, Claude, GPT, Qwen) is normal practice in China's fragmented LLM market. The framework's §9.2 orchestration formalizes what Chinese practitioners do intuitively. Western reviewers may see this as exotic; Chinese practitioners see it as Tuesday.

china_ecosystem_observations.finding: Source ecosystem is bifurcated with a subtle hierarchy
china_ecosystem_observations.detail: Western sources (SDD, AWS Atlas, PRINCE2, arXiv) are cited as primary methodological anchors; Chinese sources (Zhihu, CSDN, 网友经验) are cited as practical validation. This creates an implicit hierarchy where Western theory validates Chinese practice rather than Chinese innovation generating theory.

china_ecosystem_observations.finding: Chinese tool references are accurate and representative
china_ecosystem_observations.detail: References to Kimi, DeepSeek, GLM, baostock, akshare, 掘金量化终端 reflect real Chinese practitioner toolchains. The competitive analysis landscape (BDC2026, ETF策略) is representative of Chinese quant/ML competition culture.

unique_qwen_insights.finding: Draft status may function as cultural face-saving mechanism
unique_qwen_insights.detail: In Chinese academic/professional culture, '草案' label for extended periods can lower expectations and deflect criticism while work evolves. 5 versions in 4 days with persistent 'draft' label may function more as cultural shield than honest readiness assessment.

unique_qwen_insights.finding: Self-review machinery is more sophisticated than execution guidance
unique_qwen_insights.detail: Framework spends enormous effort on review infrastructure (SOP, meta-audit, multi-round orchestration, evidence classification) but relatively little on project execution guidance. Ratio of review infrastructure to execution guidance is inverted for what new practitioners need. This reflects the author's current preoccupation (framework IS the project being reviewed) rather than future user needs.

unique_qwen_insights.finding: Framework's self-referential review cycle is generating diminishing returns
unique_qwen_insights.detail: After 5 reviews across 4 reviewers and 3 backends, reviews have become increasingly meta. R5 finds no critical issues — remaining concerns are structural. The most productive next step is not another review but using the framework for a real project.

practical_usability.onboarding_score_rationale: 164KB main doc + 88KB JSON + 22KB SOP + 34KB meta-audit = 308KB of methodology. No Quick Start exists. Usage tiers say WHAT to use but not HOW. Assumes Claude Code CLI familiarity. Kill-test-first skill is a prerequisite the framework assumes but does not provide.

first_30_minutes_stuck_points.stuck_point: 10 Spec components (S1-S10) — which are truly mandatory? §2.2 says '必须' for S1-S4 but the relationship to §1.4 minimum tier requires cross-referencing.
Appendix A Spec template has blank fields with no worked examples. What does a good S4 (veto conditions) look like in practice?

Kill-test-first skill is referenced repeatedly as a prerequisite but is not included in the framework package. Circular dependency: need the framework to use the skill, need the skill to fill the framework.

reference_worthy_sections: §1.4 Usage Tiers — deciding what to enable
§3.2 Human Gate positions — knowing when to stop and ask
§5.3 Convergence conditions — knowing when to stop iterating
§8.4 Closure checklist — knowing when a project is done
skippable_sections: §3.6-3.7 Drift Detection full layer — overkill for short projects
§6.2 Mode 8 (Pipeline DAG) — only for branching dependency projects
§6.2 Mode 9 (Multi-Role Review) — only for formal deliverables
§9.2 Multi-round review orchestration — only when coordinating multiple AI reviewers
§9.6 Evidence classification — useful concept, impractical to apply fully
§10 Dev Log full design — concept good, implementation detail excessive for first project
§13 External references — background reading, not operational
practical_usability.quick_start_recommendation: Create a 2-3 page Quick Start showing: (1) Create Spec with S1-S4 only (15 min), (2) Write first Prompt using execution template (5 min), (3) Run Loop with convergence conditions, (4) Do Retrospect at first milestone (10 min), (5) Close with closure checklist (15 min). Everything else is upgrade path.

methodology_maturity.draft_label_assessment: Technically accurate — framework has never been used to run a complete project from L0 through L5. However, after 5 versions in 4 days, the label is starting to function as a permanent identity and review attractor rather than a temporary status.
conditions_for_stable: One complete real project using the framework from Spec through Closure (not the framework itself as a project)
Pre-registered baseline metrics (OPEN-2) collected before the project starts
Death criteria evaluation against those metrics after the project ends
At least one zero-involvement human expert review (OPEN-1 action item)
Quick Start document extracted and validated by a non-author attempting their first project
methodology_maturity.empirical_basis_assessment: Draws from 6+ completed projects, all by the same author, all using the same toolchain (Claude Code CLI). Reasonable for personal workflow documentation but not yet for generalizable methodology. Fundamental limitations: single author cognitive patterns, single toolchain coupling, no external user validation, survivor bias from completed projects only.
methodology_maturity.dogfooding_assessment: After 5 reviews across 4 reviewers and 3 backends, marginal value of each additional review round is clearly decreasing. R4 found synchronization/metadata issues (maintenance value). R5 finds no critical issues. Remaining concerns are structural and cannot be fixed by another review round.
methodology_maturity.single_most_important_need: One real, external, non-self-referential project. Use the framework to run a project that is NOT about the framework itself. This would validate/invalidate death criteria, generate real metrics, expose usability gaps, provide evidence for the '30% complexity' claim, and break the self-referential loop.

fix_verification.rationale: All 5 R4 issues genuinely fixed. JSON synced, metadata consistent, provenance annotated with honest caveats, taxonomy self-applied with transparent scope, modes integrated into decision tree and triggers. One minor regression (usage tiers JSON not updated for Mode 8/9).

cross_backend_perspective.rationale: Identified Claude Code CLI coupling depth, Chinese multi-backend culture as mainstream, source ecosystem bifurcation, draft-as-cultural-shield, and inverted review-to-execution ratio. These are genuine cross-backend insights that prior reviewers could not provide.

practical_usability.rationale: Onboarding score 4/10. 308KB total methodology, no Quick Start, circular dependency on kill-test-first skill, no worked examples. Strong templates (Spec, Prompt, Closure) but buried in accumulated content. Framework is a methodology encyclopedia needing a quick start extraction.

methodology_maturity.rationale: Draft label is honest but becoming permanent. Empirical basis from 6+ same-author projects is reasonable for personal documentation but not generalizable methodology. Dogfooding has reached diminishing returns. Single most important need: one real external project.

comparison_to_r4_grade: Improved from C+ (5.43/10) to B- (6.50/10). All 5 R4 issues genuinely fixed with no regressions. Remaining gap is structural (empirical basis, usability, scope control) rather than synchronization or metadata.

key_findings.finding: Framework needs a Quick Start extraction
key_findings.detail: 308KB of methodology with no runnable first-project guide. Usage tiers say what to use but not how. Onboarding score: 4/10.

key_findings.finding: Self-referential review cycle has reached diminishing returns
key_findings.detail: 5 reviews across 4 reviewers and 3 backends. R5 finds no critical issues. Most productive next step is a real project, not another review.

key_findings.finding: Claude Code CLI coupling is deeper than acknowledged
key_findings.detail: Framework's executable form is Claude Code-specific at every layer. Conceptual architecture is tool-agnostic but implementation is not.

key_findings.finding: Companion SOP and meta-audit still scoped to v1.4
key_findings.detail: Not contradictory (they explicitly state v1.4 scope) but represents accumulated maintenance debt.

key_findings.finding: JSON usage tiers do not include Mode 8/9
key_findings.detail: Minor integration gap — new modes are in patterns section but not in usage tier upgrade lists.

recommendations: Create a 2-3 page Quick Start document showing minimum viable workflow for a first project
Run one real, non-self-referential project using the framework from Spec through Closure
Add a Tool Adaptation Guide distinguishing conceptual architecture from Claude Code-specific implementation
Update companion SOP and meta-audit to v1.5 scope or explicitly mark version lag
Add Mode 8/9 to JSON usage_tiers upgrade lists
Include at least one complete worked example (Spec + Prompt + Retrospect) from a real project
